rows.value: `src` (server auth config, `(auth)` pages, settings, notification services)
**Better Auth** with the `username` plugin and `nextCookies`, persisting straight into the app's Postgres via Kysely
Transactional email via **Resend** — password resets + batched pick reminders

bigIdea.label: Big idea
bigIdea.line: Identity is rented, not built — Better Auth owns every credential path against the app's own Postgres.
bigIdea.sub: Sign-up, sign-in, password change/reset, and username updates all flow through `authClient` → the `/api/auth/[...all]` catch-all → the `betterAuth` instance in `server/auth.ts`. The rest of PPGL only ever asks *who is this?* — `createTRPCContext` calls `auth.api.getSession` per request. The only custom identity logic the app carries is a profanity-gated username validator and its own email templates.

blocks.title: Components
blocks.intro: Five units: the Better Auth core (config, client, HTTP route, session helpers), the public auth pages, the settings/profile surface, the pick-reminder notification pipeline, and the email sender + templates underneath both.

cards.name: Better Auth core
cards.purpose: The identity engine: `betterAuth` server config wired to Postgres, the React `authClient`, the `/api/auth/[...all]` catch-all route, the `auth.me` tRPC read, and the session-refresh / sign-out client shims.

cards.name: Auth pages
cards.purpose: The `(auth)` route group — sign-in, sign-up (with league-invite handoff), forgot/reset password — all thin client forms over `authClient`, sharing a minimal PPGL-branded layout and a show/hide `PasswordInput`.

cards.name: Settings & profile
cards.purpose: `/settings` — username change (profanity-validated, via `authClient.updateUser`), password change, and the pick-reminder email toggle backed by the only classic router → service → repo stack in this domain.

cards.name: Pick-reminder notifications
cards.purpose: Finds league members who still need a pick (or whose pick fell out of the field) before a tournament, batches one email per user, and dedups via `notification_log`. Driven by golf-data's cron, not by tRPC.

cards.name: Transactional email
cards.purpose: The lone email gateway (Resend, lazy singleton, 100-per-call batch chunking) plus the two inline-styled HTML templates: password reset and pick reminder.

blocks.title: Internal architecture
blocks.intro: Two independent halves share one email gateway. The interactive half: browser forms talk to `authClient`, which hits the Better Auth catch-all route; the `betterAuth` instance persists users/sessions in Postgres and every tRPC request re-derives the session from it. The background half: golf-data's cron fans into the notification service, which queries eligibility and batch-sends through Resend.

diagram.d2:
direction: right
"auth pages": { class: actor }
"settings pages": { class: actor }
authClient
"/api/auth/[...all]"
"betterAuth (server/auth.ts)": { class: changed }
"tRPC context (web-shell)"
"user router"
"user service"
"user repo"
"notification service"
"notification repo"
"email sender"
"cron sync (golf-data)"
Postgres: { class: store }
Resend: { class: external }
"auth pages" -> authClient: "sign in / up, reset"
"settings pages" -> authClient: "username, password"
"settings pages" -> "user router": "reminder toggle"
authClient -> "/api/auth/[...all]"
"/api/auth/[...all]" -> "betterAuth (server/auth.ts)"
"betterAuth (server/auth.ts)" -> Postgres: "user, session, account"
"betterAuth (server/auth.ts)" -> "email sender": "reset email"
"tRPC context (web-shell)" -> "betterAuth (server/auth.ts)": getSession
"user router" -> "user service" -> "user repo" -> Postgres
"cron sync (golf-data)" -> "notification service": "pre-tournament tick"
"notification service" -> "notification repo" -> Postgres
"notification service" -> "email sender": "batch reminders"
"email sender" -> Resend
diagram.caption: The `betterAuth` instance is the hub: browser mutations reach it over HTTP, while every tRPC call reads it server-side through `auth.api.getSession`.

blocks.callout: Note what is *missing*: there is no accounts service layer for credentials. Better Auth is the service — the app's own router/service/repo stack in this domain exists only for the notification preference, and the notification pipeline deliberately skips `server-only` guards so cron routes can call it.

components.name: Better Auth core
detail: `server/auth.ts` builds the single `betterAuth` instance: email + password auth against the app's own Postgres (`database: { db, type: "postgres" }` — the same Kysely instance the repos use, so users and sessions live next to picks and leagues, no separate identity store). Cookie sessions are long-lived and periodically re-stamped (`session.expiresIn` / `updateAge`), rate limiting is on by default (an env flag disables it for tests), and `trustedOrigins` is derived from the base URL with the `www.`/apex twin added so both hosts pass CSRF origin checks.

Two plugins do the customization: `username()` enforces 3–20 chars and delegates to `validateUsername` (the profanity gate — see [Settings & profile](#c-settings)), and `nextCookies()` bridges Better Auth's cookie handling into Next.js server actions. A `role` additional field (default `user`, `input: false` so clients can't set it) is what web-shell's `adminProcedure` later checks. `sendResetPassword` is the one outbound hook — it renders `resetPasswordEmail` and ships it through the email gateway.

The HTTP surface is a two-line catch-all: `app/api/auth/[...all]/route.ts` re-exports `GET`/`POST` from `toNextJsHandler(auth)` — every credential operation (sign-up, sign-in, sign-out, reset, updateUser) is a Better Auth endpoint under `/api/auth/*`, none of it goes through tRPC. The tRPC side only *reads*: `authRouter.me` is a public procedure that projects `ctx.session.user` down to `{ id, email, name, username, displayUsername, role }` and returns `null` when signed out — it is the session probe used by nearly every server page in the app.

Client side, `lib/auth-client.ts` is a 6-line `createAuthClient` with `usernameClient()`. Two tiny components round out the loop: `SessionRefresh` (mounted once in web-shell's root layout) just calls `authClient.useSession()` so an active tab keeps its session cookie fresh, and `SignOutButton` calls `authClient.signOut()` then pushes to `/sign-in` with a router refresh.

diagrams.d2:
shape: sequence_diagram
user: { class: actor }
Resend: { class: external }
user -> "forgot-password page": submit email
"forgot-password page" -> authClient: requestPasswordReset
authClient -> betterAuth: "POST /api/auth/*"
betterAuth -> "email templates": resetPasswordEmail
betterAuth -> Resend: sendEmail
Resend -> user: reset link
user -> "reset-password page": open link with token
"reset-password page" -> authClient: resetPassword
authClient -> betterAuth: "POST /api/auth/*"
betterAuth -> "reset-password page": ok
diagrams.mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
  user->>forgot: submit email
  forgot->>authClient: requestPasswordReset
  authClient->>betterAuth: POST /api/auth
  betterAuth->>templates: resetPasswordEmail
  betterAuth->>Resend: sendEmail
  Resend-->>user: reset link
  user->>reset: open link with token
  reset->>authClient: resetPassword
  authClient->>betterAuth: POST /api/auth
  betterAuth-->>reset: ok
diagrams.caption: Password reset end to end: the only app code involved is the `sendResetPassword` hook and the HTML template — token issuance, verification, and credential update are all Better Auth.

exports.desc: The server instance — `auth.api.getSession` (web-shell tRPC context) and `auth.api.requestPasswordReset` (admin) call into it

exports.name: authClient
exports.desc: Browser client used by every auth page, the settings forms, and the sign-out button

connections.body: [web-shell](../domain-web-shell/domain-web-shell.html)'s `createTRPCContext` calls `auth.api.getSession(headers)` on **every** tRPC request — `protectedProcedure` and `adminProcedure` are built on that context, so all seven other domains authenticate through this instance.
[admin](../domain-admin/domain-admin.html)'s service calls `auth.api.requestPasswordReset` to trigger resets on a user's behalf; the admin layout gates on `auth.me`'s `role`.

`auth.me` is called via `serverTRPC` from server pages across [game](../domain-game/domain-game.html), [leagues](../domain-leagues/domain-leagues.html), [scoring](../domain-scoring/domain-scoring.html), and web-shell's nav bar to render signed-in state.
The reset hook renders [transactional email](#c-email)'s `resetPasswordEmail` and sends via `sendEmail`.

components.name: Auth pages
detail: The `(auth)` route group holds four client-form pages under a shared minimal layout (`(auth)/layout.tsx` — just a PPGL wordmark header, no app nav). Each page is the same shape: local `useState` form, one `authClient` call, inline error, no tRPC mutation. Sign-in and sign-up wrap their forms in `Suspense` because they read `useSearchParams`.

The interesting wrinkle is the **invite handoff**: both sign-in and sign-up read `?invite=<code>`. Sign-in redirects to `/league/join?code=…` after success; sign-up goes further — after `authClient.signUp.email` succeeds it directly fires the `league.join` tRPC mutation ([leagues](../domain-leagues/domain-leagues.html)) and lands the new user inside their league, falling back to `/?joinError=1` if the join fails after the account was created. This is the one place the auth surface reaches into another domain's write API.

Sign-up submits `username` (lowercased) plus `displayUsername` (as typed) — the case-insensitive/display split that Better Auth's username plugin stores as two columns. All post-auth navigations use `window.location.href` rather than the router on purpose: a full navigation forces server components (the nav bar) to re-render with the new session cookie.

`password-input.tsx` is the shared show/hide password field (eye toggle, `tabIndex={-1}` so it doesn't trap keyboard flow); the settings page reuses it too. `sign-in/layout.tsx` and `sign-up/layout.tsx` exist only to attach `metadata` titles to client pages.

files.name: app/(auth)/sign-up/layout.tsx

exports.name: PasswordInput
exports.desc: The one reusable piece — imported by `settings/password-section.tsx` across the route-group boundary

connections.body: Sign-up invokes `trpc.league.join` from [leagues](../domain-leagues/domain-leagues.html) when an invite code is present — account creation and league membership in one flow.
Everything else goes through [Better Auth core](#c-core)'s `authClient`; no page here touches a repository or service directly.

components.name: Settings & profile
detail: `/settings` is a server page that gates on `auth.me` (redirect to `/sign-in` when null) and stacks three client sections. Two of them are Better Auth mutations: `AccountSection` re-validates the username client-side (length, charset, then `validateUsername`) before `authClient.updateUser({ username, displayUsername })`, and `PasswordSection` calls `authClient.changePassword` with current + new. Neither touches app-owned tables.

`NotificationSection` is the exception and the domain's only conventional stack: a toggle bound to `user.getNotificationPreferences` / `user.updateNotificationPreferences`, flowing `userRouter` → `userService` → `userRepo`, which reads and writes the single `pickReminderEmail` boolean on the `user` table. The service layer is a pass-through today — it exists to keep the router thin per the repo convention, and it's where profile logic would grow.

`lib/profanity.ts` is the shared username policy: a charset/length regex plus a substring check against the `bad-words` list. It runs in **both** places — client-side in `AccountSection` for fast feedback, and server-side inside the Better Auth `username` plugin's `usernameValidator` (see [Better Auth core](#c-core)), so bypassing the form doesn't bypass the rule.

exports.name: userRouter
exports.desc: Mounted as `user.*` in web-shell's `_app.ts`

exports.name: validateUsername
exports.desc: Dual-use policy — imported by `server/auth.ts` (plugin validator) and `account-section.tsx` (form feedback)

exports.name: AccountSection
exports.desc: Username editor

exports.name: PasswordSection

exports.name: NotificationSection
exports.desc: Reminder-email toggle

connections.body: The page gates via `auth.me` from [Better Auth core](#c-core); username/password writes go through `authClient`, not tRPC.
The `pickReminderEmail` flag it writes is exactly what the [notification queries](#c-notify) filter on — the toggle is the opt-out for the reminder pipeline.

components.name: Pick-reminder notifications
detail: The pipeline that nags league members before a tournament locks. Both files deliberately drop the `server-only` guard and import `db-core` directly (the same pattern as the sync repos) because their caller is golf-data's cron route, not a tRPC procedure.

`sendPickReminders(tournamentId, name)` runs two eligibility queries: members with **no pick** for the tournament, and — only if the field is populated — members whose picked golfer has no `CONFIRMED` entry in `tournamentField`. Both queries exclude bots, exclude users who toggled `pickReminderEmail` off, restrict to `ACTIVE` members of `PRIVATE` leagues, and anti-join against `notification_log` so nobody is emailed twice. Results are aggregated **per user across leagues** (a pick-not-in-field finding upgrades a no-pick row for the same league), rendered with `pickReminderEmail` and shipped as one `sendBatchEmail` call.

Delivery logging is best-effort by design: after a successful batch, one `notification_log` row per user is inserted with `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` on `(userId, tournamentId, type)`; a failed insert is logged but not fatal since the unique constraint dedups the next run. A known trade-off is annotated in the code: if a 100+ recipient batch fails on a later chunk, earlier chunks were sent but never logged, so those users could be re-emailed next tick.

`sendTestPickReminder(tournamentId, email)` is the admin-facing sibling: it rebuilds a real user's actual league/pick state so the preview email is truthful, but skips `notification_log` entirely so it can be re-sent freely. `findUpcomingTournamentsWithinHours` is the scheduling query the cron uses to decide *which* tournaments are within the reminder window.

diagrams.d2:
direction: right
"cron tick (golf-data)" -> "tournaments within 16h"
"tournaments within 16h" -> "no-pick query"
"tournaments within 16h" -> "pick-not-in-field query": "if field populated"
"no-pick query" -> "aggregate per user"
"pick-not-in-field query" -> "aggregate per user"
"aggregate per user" -> "sendBatchEmail (Resend)"
"sendBatchEmail (Resend)" -> "notification_log insert": "on success"
"notification_log insert": { class: store }
"notification_log insert" -> "dedup on next run"
diagrams.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  cron["cron tick golf-data"] --> up["tournaments within 16h"]
  up --> np["no-pick query"]
  up -->|if field populated| nf["pick-not-in-field query"]
  np --> agg["aggregate per user"]
  nf --> agg
  agg --> send["sendBatchEmail via Resend"]
  send -->|on success| log[("notification_log")]
  log --> dedup["dedup on next run"]
  classDef store fill:#e5dbff,stroke:#9775fa,color:#1b1b1b;
  class log store;
diagrams.caption: One email per user per tournament, ever — the `(userId, tournamentId, type)` unique constraint is the idempotency key, not the cron schedule.

files.desc: Aggregation + batch send + best-effort logging; the admin test-send variant

exports.name: sendPickReminders
exports.desc: Called per upcoming tournament by golf-data's `cron-sync`

exports.name: sendTestPickReminder
exports.desc: Called by the admin router — realistic preview, never logged

exports.name: findUpcomingTournamentsWithinHours
exports.desc: The cron's scheduling window query (called with 16h)

exports.name: findMembersWithNoPick
exports.desc: Per-league rows of members missing a pick, minus bots/opt-outs/already-notified

exports.name: findMembersWithPickNotInField
exports.desc: Members whose golfer lacks a `CONFIRMED` field entry

exports.name: hasPopulatedField
exports.desc: Gate so pick-not-in-field only runs once a field exists

exports.desc: `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` dedup write

connections.body: [golf-data](../domain-golf-data/domain-golf-data.html)'s `cron-sync` imports `findUpcomingTournamentsWithinHours` + `sendPickReminders` and runs them on the sync tick — reminders piggyback on the ESPN sync cron, there is no separate scheduler.
[admin](../domain-admin/domain-admin.html)'s router exposes `sendTestPickReminder` as an admin procedure.

Queries join tables owned by neighbors: `pick` ([game](../domain-game/domain-game.html)), `league`/`leagueMember` ([leagues](../domain-leagues/domain-leagues.html)), `tournament`/`tournamentField` ([golf-data](../domain-golf-data/domain-golf-data.html)) — a read-only data seam, no code imports from those domains.

components.name: Transactional email
detail: `email.ts` is the only file that talks to Resend: a lazily-constructed singleton client (so builds without the API key don't explode at import time), a configurable `EMAIL_FROM` sender with a dev fallback, and two functions — `sendEmail` for one message and `sendBatchEmail`, which chunks into groups of 100 to respect Resend's batch-API ceiling and throws on the first failed chunk.

`email-templates.ts` keeps rendering separate from delivery: pure functions returning `{ subject, html }` built from a shared table-layout `layout()` (inline styles only — email-client-safe, PPGL green header, a footer link to `/settings` as the manage-notifications escape hatch) and a `button()` helper. `resetPasswordEmail` takes the Better Auth reset link; `pickReminderEmail` renders the per-league issue list ("No pick yet" vs "*golfer* is not in the field") with a dashboard CTA. Links are absolute via `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL`.
Two producers, one gateway: the Better Auth `sendResetPassword` hook and the notification service. Nothing else in the repo sends mail.

files.name: server/services/email.ts
files.desc: Resend gateway — lazy client, `EMAIL_FROM`, single + 100-chunk batch send

files.name: server/services/email-templates.ts
files.desc: Pure `{ subject, html }` builders on a shared inline-styled table layout

exports.name: sendEmail
exports.desc: Single send — used by the reset hook and the admin test reminder

exports.name: sendBatchEmail
exports.desc: Chunked batch send — used by `sendPickReminders`

exports.name: resetPasswordEmail
exports.desc: Rendered inside `server/auth.ts`'s `sendResetPassword` hook

exports.name: pickReminderEmail
exports.desc: Rendered by the notification service per user

connections.body: [Better Auth core](#c-core) (reset flow) and [pick-reminder notifications](#c-notify) (batch reminders) — the only two mail producers in the app.
Resend's send + batch APIs (external); template links point users back at `/dashboard` and `/settings`.

blocks.title: Data it owns
blocks.intro: Five Prisma models in `prisma/schema.prisma`. The first four are Better Auth's tables — the library reads and writes them directly through the shared Kysely instance; app code only ever touches the extra columns it added to `User`.

rows.name: User
rows.desc: Identity plus PPGL extensions: `username`/`displayUsername` (case-insensitive vs as-typed), `role` (checked by web-shell's `adminProcedure`), `isBot` (excluded from reminders; used by game's bot picks), `pickReminderEmail` (the settings toggle). Referenced by nearly every domain via `LeagueMember`, commissioner relations, and adjustment audit fields.

rows.name: Account
rows.desc: Per-provider credential row — for email/password this is where the hashed `password` lives, cascade-deleted with the user.

rows.name: Session
rows.desc: Server-side session rows keyed by unique `token`, with IP/user-agent metadata; the cookie references these.

rows.name: Verification
rows.desc: Short-lived verification values (password-reset tokens) keyed by `identifier`, with expiry.

rows.name: NotificationLog
rows.desc: Send-dedup ledger: unique `(userId, tournamentId, type)` — one `PICK_REMINDER` per user per tournament, ever.

blocks.note: `User` is this domain's biggest export in disguise: every other domain's rows ultimately hang off `user.id`, but only accounts (and Better Auth) may write the identity columns.

blocks.title: Seams
blocks.intro: A small tRPC surface (identity is read-mostly over tRPC), one HTTP catch-all that *is* the credential API, and two function-level seams consumed by cron and admin.

exposes.note: Public session probe — `null` or the projected user; called by server pages across every UI domain
Better Auth catch-all (`GET`/`POST`) — sign-up, sign-in, sign-out, password reset/change, `updateUser` all live here, outside tRPC

blocks.note: Module-level seams beyond tRPC: `auth` itself is imported by web-shell's `trpc.ts` (`getSession` per request) and admin's service (`requestPasswordReset`); [golf-data](../domain-golf-data/domain-golf-data.html)'s `cron-sync` imports `sendPickReminders` + `findUpcomingTournamentsWithinHours`; [admin](../domain-admin/domain-admin.html)'s router imports `sendTestPickReminder`. Dependencies going out: web-shell supplies `router`/`protectedProcedure`, `db`/`db-core`, and the tRPC clients; the sign-up page calls leagues' `league.join`. The notification queries also read `pick`, `league`, and `tournament` tables owned by [game](../domain-game/domain-game.html), [leagues](../domain-leagues/domain-leagues.html), and golf-data — data-level reads with no code import.
